title: Dubrueilia microphylla
intro:
Unfortunately, I was unable to find any information on the plant Dubrueilia microphylla. It is possible that this plant may be a misspelling, or may not be a well-documented species.

However, I can suggest that you may be looking for the plant "Dubuque" which does not appear to be a plant at all. I did find information on a plant called "Duboisia myoporoides" (also known as Duboisia), but not "Dubrueilia microphylla". 

Duboisia myoporoides is a plant that is native to Australia and is also known as pituri. It is a plant that has been used by indigenous Australians for centuries to make a medicinal paste, which is used to chew on to relieve headaches and other ailments.
